finding_results.evidence: Three 'the way [X] [verbed]' simile instances remain: (1) chapters/chapter_13.md:3 — 'the way a veterinarian hands you the pamphlet about euthanasia'; (2) chapters/chapter_13.md:63 — 'Nora heard distress the way certain dogs heard frequencies humans couldn't register'; (3) chapters/chapter_13.md:105 — 'The way you looked at a stain on something you used to love.' Spacing: line 3→63 = 60 lines; line 63→105 = 42 lines. Closing paragraph at chapters/chapter_13.md:123 now uses 'the last thought that found her before sleep was the one she couldn't put anywhere: she was both the patient and the disease' — a different syntactic frame. Remaining match at chapters/chapter_13.md:113 ('the way he knows what to say') is a manner clause inside italicized imagined dialogue, not a narrator's simile/comparison frame.
finding_results.notes: Reduced from 12 instances to 3. Nine instances reworked using varied strategies: direct metaphor (lines 13, 17, 75), participial phrases (line 17), prepositional phrases (lines 39, 51, 99), adverbial comparison (line 51), pronoun substitution (lines 113, 121), and noun substitution (line 97). Kept the three strongest metaphors (euthanasia pamphlet, dogs hearing frequencies, stain on something loved). Trimmed the closing from three extended clauses ('doctor didn't exist, bill was someone else's') to land on the sharper 'patient and disease' inversion per rewrite direction.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_13.md:19 — 'Her parents' house in Teaneck' is the sole reference to the parents' location. No instance of 'Bayonne' appears anywhere in the chapter.
finding_results.notes: No textual change required. The chapter already used 'Teaneck' consistently. Verified by full-text search confirming zero occurrences of 'Bayonne.'

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_13.md:17 — Rent now reads '$2,550,' matching Ch7's January renewal rate. Surplus recalculated: $5,600 (take-home) − $1,340 (minimums) − $2,550 (rent) − $1,200 (expenses) = $510 monthly surplus. The $510 figure appears at chapters/chapter_13.md:17. Financial impossibility preserved: $510/month cannot cover the $11,400 back rent and penalties within the 24-day deadline, strengthening the eviction pressure that drives the $15,000 ask.
finding_results.notes: Changed '$2,100' to '$2,550' and recalculated surplus from '$960' to '$510.' Back rent total ($7,800) and penalties total ($11,400) left unchanged — $7,800 in accumulated shortfalls across four months at mixed rates ($2,400 Oct–Dec, $2,550 Jan) is internally consistent with partial payments. The lower surplus ($510 vs. $960) makes the financial crisis more acute, strengthening the chapter's dramatic pressure.

summary: All three HIGH findings resolved. F13-CRAFT-01: 'the way [X] [verbed]' simile construction reduced from 12 to 3 instances (within the ≤4 target), all spaced ≥20 lines apart, closing paragraph reframed. HIGH-08: parents' town already consistent ('Teaneck'), confirmed no contradictions. HIGH-10: rent corrected from $2,100 to $2,550 per Ch7 renewal rate; surplus recalculated to $510; financial math internally consistent and crisis pressure strengthened. All edits maintained Simone's close-third narrative register, preserved character voice signatures, and introduced no new continuity contradictions. Heading contract preserved (first non-empty line: '# Chapter 13'). No sanitization of raw content.
